water.name: Butt Creek and Butt Valley Reservoir Powerhouse Outfall

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Butt Creek
reaches.fromDesc: Butt Creek above and below Butt Valley Reservoir (Humbug Valley drainage, Plumas Co.)
reaches.toDesc: Butt Creek above and below Butt Valley Reservoir

reaches.name: Butt Valley Reservoir powerhouse outfall
reaches.fromDesc: Butt Valley Powerhouse outfall (near Prattville, north end of Butt Valley Reservoir)
reaches.toDesc: Marker adjacent to Ponderosa Flat Campground

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50(b)(20) — Butt Creek and Butt Valley Reservoir Powerhouse Outfall
sources.quotedText: (20) Butt Creek and Butt Valley Reservoir Powerhouse Outfall (Plumas Co.). (A) Butt Creek. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout (B) Butt Valley Reservoir powerhouse outfall, from the powerhouse downstream to a marker adjacent to Ponderosa Flat Campground. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50(a) — General provisions for the special-regulations trout water list
sources.quotedText: (3) Daily bag and possession limits, unless otherwise noted, mean the total number of trout. (4) Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: Butt Valley Reservoir — Plumas County California
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: HomeTownLocator — GNIS Feature Detail: Butt Valley Reservoir (GNIS ID 257757), Reservoir, Plumas County, CA
sources.quotedText: Feature Type: Cultural Class: Reservoir County: Plumas County Latitude: 40.1428609 Longitude: -121.1717502

groups.humanSummary: Butt Creek and Butt Valley Reservoir Powerhouse Outfall: two separately regulated segments — (A) Butt Creek itself, open Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30 with a 5-trout limit; (B) the powerhouse outfall channel from the powerhouse down to a marker at Ponderosa Flat Campground, open Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day of February with a 2-trout daily / 4-trout possession limit.
groups.verbatimText: Butt Creek and Butt Valley Reservoir Powerhouse Outfall (Plumas Co.). (A) Butt Creek. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout (B) Butt Valley Reservoir powerhouse outfall, from the powerhouse downstream to a marker adjacent to Ponderosa Flat Campground. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Butt Creek open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30.
regulations.verbatimText: (A) Butt Creek. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30.

parameters.counting_rule: Possession limit equals one daily bag limit; per 7.50(a)(4), unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 5 in possession, on Butt Creek.

parameters.note: Season spans the calendar year-end (late May through the last day of the following February).

regulations.humanSummary: Butt Valley Reservoir powerhouse outfall (powerhouse down to the Ponderosa Flat Campground marker) open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through the last day of February.
regulations.verbatimText: (B) Butt Valley Reservoir powerhouse outfall, from the powerhouse downstream to a marker adjacent to Ponderosa Flat Campground. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag limit, 4 in possession, on the powerhouse outfall segment.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
